events.title: Israeli Airstrikes Kill Seven in South Lebanon
events.desc: An Israeli airstrike on the town of Saksakiyeh in southern Lebanon resulted in at least seven fatalities, including a child, and 15 injuries. The Israeli military stated they targeted Hezbollah militants who were allegedly planning attacks against Israeli soldiers. The military is currently reviewing details regarding civilian casualties.

events.title: Iran Threatens US Sites Amidst Gulf Tanker Incidents
events.desc: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has issued a stern warning, threatening to target US sites in the Middle East and 'enemy ships' if its tankers are attacked. This statement follows recent US strikes on two Iranian tankers in the Gulf of Oman, escalating tensions in the region.

events.title: Israel Pounds Lebanon Despite Truce, Nine Killed
events.desc: Israel conducted extensive strikes across Lebanon on Saturday, killing at least nine individuals in the south, according to authorities. Attacks also targeted a highway near Beirut. These strikes represent some of the most intense since a three-week ceasefire began, though daily exchanges of fire persist.

events.title: US and Iran No Closer to Ending War as Gulf Clashes Flare
events.desc: The United States and Iran remain distant from reaching an agreement to end their conflict, despite a fragile ceasefire. Recent clashes in and around the Strait of Hormuz mark the most significant escalation since the truce began a month ago, with the UAE also experiencing renewed attacks.

events.title: UK Deploys Warship HMS Dragon to Middle East Amid Hormuz Concerns
events.desc: The United Kingdom has announced the deployment of the warship HMS Dragon to the Middle East. The vessel is being positioned in preparation for a potential multinational operation to ensure shipping safety in the Strait of Hormuz, should conditions permit.

events.title: US Imposes Sanctions on Entities Aiding Iran's Weapons Sector
events.desc: The U.S. Treasury has imposed sanctions on ten individuals and companies, including those based in China and Hong Kong, for allegedly assisting Iran's military in acquiring weapons and materials for its Shahed drones. This action precedes President Trump's visit to China.

events.title: Israeli Military Investigates Soldier's Act Against Religious Statue in Lebanon
events.desc: The Israeli military is investigating an incident where a soldier was photographed disrespecting a Virgin Mary statue in Lebanon by placing a cigarette in its mouth. This follows a previous incident involving a Christian figure, raising concerns about conduct in occupied territories.

events.title: Two Waves of Bombing Detailed in New Videos of Iran School Strike
events.desc: Newly verified videos provide further details on two waves of bombing that struck an IRGC compound and an adjacent school in Minab, Iran, in late February. Released by Iran's Ministry of Foreign Affairs, the footage shows multiple missile impacts.

events.title: Satellite Images Show Suspected Oil Slick Near Iran's Kharg Island
events.desc: Satellite imagery has captured a significant oil slick, estimated to span dozens of square kilometers, in the vicinity of Iran's Kharg Island. The exact cause and origin of the slick are under investigation.

events.title: Three Police Officers Killed in Car Bomb Attack in Northwest Pakistan
events.desc: A car bomb attack in Bannu, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan, has resulted in the deaths of three police officers. The bomber detonated an explosives-laden vehicle near a security post, with several other individuals also reportedly injured.

events.title: Russia Supports US-Iran Talks to Prevent Hostilities
events.desc: Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov emphasized the need to support ongoing negotiations between the US and Iran to prevent a resurgence of hostilities in the Middle East. The comments were made during a telephone conversation with his UAE counterpart.

events.title: Iran Warns of Attacks on US Sites, Awaits US Response
events.desc: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards have threatened to target US sites in the Middle East if their tankers face attack. This warning comes as Washington awaits Tehran's response to its latest negotiation proposals, amidst ongoing tensions and recent US strikes on Iranian tankers.

events.title: Israeli Settlers Force Re-burial of Palestinian Man in West Bank
events.desc: Israeli settlers allegedly forced a Palestinian family in the occupied West Bank to exhume the body of their recently deceased father and rebury him. The incident occurred near a settlement re-established by the current Israeli government, and the family states the burial had all necessary permits.

events.title: Lebanon Reports 39 Deaths from Israeli Strikes
events.desc: Lebanon's authorities report that Israeli strikes have resulted in 39 fatalities. This comes despite a ceasefire agreement announced last month, indicating continued tensions and exchanges of fire between Israel and Hezbollah.

events.title: Iran Accuses US of 'Reckless Military Adventure'
events.desc: Iranian official Abbas Araghchi has accused the United States of engaging in 'reckless military adventures,' suggesting that US attacks occur specifically when diplomatic solutions are being considered. This statement highlights the complex and often contradictory nature of the ongoing conflict.
